LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS MELISSA VINCENT AND WILLIAM VINCENT'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS TIMELY, DEFENDANTS PROVIDE A PROPOSED PLEADING, AND MR. BROCKBANK AND MS. GRIFFIN EXPLAIN HOW THE DEFAULT WAS ENTERED AS THE RESULT OF EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. DEFENDANTS HAVE LEAVE TO FILE THEIR PROPOSED ANSWER. THE COURT DENIES THE PARTIES' REQUEST FOR FEES/SANCTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD A. KRAMER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN CSR6185.

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Aug-13-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: NICOLET CORLISS, ESQ., 415.433.4800, FOR PLAINTIFF BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES.
SUSAN GRIFFIN, ESQ., 415.397.2600, FOR DEFENDANT MELISSA VINCENT.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs MELISSA VINCENT and WILLIAM VINCENT's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT is granted. The motion is timely, defendants provide a proposed pleading, and Mr. Brockbank and Ms. Griffin explain how the default was entered as the result of excusable neglect. Defendants have leave to file their proposed answer. The Court denies the parties' request for fees/sanctions. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard A. Kramer, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR6185.
